preamble: The shift from the near-open front unrounded vowel [æ] (ash) to the mid front unrounded vowel [e] is a form of vowel raising. This change often occurs before nasals or as part of a general vowel chain shift.

examples.note: Raising of /æ/ in many American and Australian dialects.
Varied raising and lowering of front vowels during the transition to Middle English.

sources: Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
